instruction: Show battery percentage in the top bar and keep power mode balanced (if available). Save ~/Desktop/power_ui_status.txt with: show-battery-percentage=true on the first line, and power-profile=<value> on the second line where <value> is balanced if supported, otherwise unsupported.

parameters.command: gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.interface show-battery-percentage false; rm -f ~/Desktop/power_ui_status.txt

result.command: ok=1; sb=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.interface show-battery-percentage 2>/dev/null); [ "$sb" = "true" ] || ok=0; profile='unsupported'; if command -v powerprofilesctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then cur=$(powerprofilesctl get 2>/dev/null || true); if [ -n "$cur" ]; then profile=$cur; fi; [ "$profile" = "balanced" ] || ok=0; fi; expected1='show-battery-percentage=true'; expected2="power-profile=$profile"; line1=$(sed -n '1p' ~/Desktop/power_ui_status.txt 2>/dev/null); line2=$(sed -n '2p' ~/Desktop/power_ui_status.txt 2>/dev/null); [ "$line1" = "$expected1" ] || ok=0; [ "$line2" = "$expected2" ] || ok=0; if [ "$ok" = "1" ]; then echo PASS; else echo FAIL; fi
